title: List Nginx Listen Directives
hook: The site was configured, but the port was not.
problem: You need to see which Nginx server blocks claim ports before chasing DNS or app errors.
starting_state: A fixture contains multiple Nginx server block files.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command searches Nginx config files for listen directives.
when_to_use: Use when a site answers on the wrong port, misses HTTPS, or has competing server blocks.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume a listen line proves the service is actually bound; check sockets separately when needed.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Config filenames and line numbers containing listen directives.

shorts.title: Check what Nginx listens on.
shorts.voiceover: Before blaming the app, list the listen directives. A missing 443 or duplicate default server can explain the outage.
shorts.caption: Ports first. App later.

linkedin.question: When Nginx serves the wrong site, do you check listen lines or server names first?
linkedin.cta: Map Nginx ports before editing configs.

youtube.description: List Nginx listen directives from static config files.

title: Find the Nginx Default Server
hook: The wrong site answered because it was the fallback.
problem: You need to find which Nginx server block is marked as the default.
starting_state: A fixture contains Nginx configs where one server may be the fallback for unmatched hosts.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command searches for default_server flags.
when_to_use: Use when requests without a matching host header land on an unexpected site.
when_not_to_use: Do not treat the default server as the cause when the Host header or DNS is still unknown.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Config paths and line numbers where default_server appears.

shorts.title: Find the fallback server.
shorts.voiceover: If Nginx serves the wrong site, look for default_server. That line decides where unmatched requests go.
shorts.caption: Find the fallback before guessing.

linkedin.question: Have you debugged the wrong Nginx site because the fallback answered?
linkedin.cta: Check default_server before changing host rules.

youtube.description: Find the Nginx default server in config files.

title: Show Nginx Include Lines
hook: The config was valid; it just was not included.
problem: You need to see which files the main Nginx config is expected to include.
starting_state: A fixture contains an Nginx main config plus included config directories.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command searches for include directives.
when_to_use: Use when an edited config file appears to be ignored.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume every include glob matches files; inspect the target path when output looks suspicious.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Include directives with config paths and line numbers.

shorts.title: Is that config included?
shorts.voiceover: Editing a file does not matter if Nginx never includes it. Search the include chain before changing more config.
shorts.caption: Included config is active config.

linkedin.question: What is your quickest way to prove a config file is in the include path?

youtube.description: Trace Nginx include directives from static config files.

title: Map Nginx Roots and Aliases
hook: The URL was right. The filesystem path was not.
problem: You need to find which document roots and aliases Nginx config points at.
starting_state: A fixture contains Nginx server and location blocks with root or alias directives.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command lists filesystem path directives from config.
when_to_use: Use when static assets, uploads, or index files return 404 unexpectedly.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume root and alias behave the same inside location blocks.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Config paths and lines containing root or alias directives.

shorts.title: Find where Nginx serves files.
shorts.voiceover: A 404 can be a path problem, not an app problem. List root and alias directives before moving files.
shorts.caption: Map URL paths to disk paths.

linkedin.question: Do you check root and alias before debugging static-file 404s?
linkedin.cta: Map Nginx file paths before changing permissions.

youtube.description: Find Nginx root and alias directives.

title: Map Nginx Proxy Targets
hook: Nginx was healthy. It was proxying to the wrong place.
problem: You need to see where Nginx forwards proxied requests.
starting_state: A fixture contains Nginx reverse-proxy configuration.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command lists proxy_pass targets.
when_to_use: Use when 502s, stale apps, or wrong backends suggest a proxy target problem.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume the upstream process is running just because the config points to it.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Config paths and proxy_pass target URLs.

shorts.title: Where is Nginx proxying?
shorts.voiceover: For 502s, check the proxy target before restarting services. Nginx may be forwarding to the wrong port.
shorts.caption: Find proxy_pass before restarting.

linkedin.question: For a 502, do you inspect proxy_pass before checking the app process?

title: Show Enabled Apache Sites
hook: The Apache config existed. The enabled symlink did not.
problem: You need to see which Apache virtual host files are enabled.
starting_state: A fixture contains Apache sites-available and sites-enabled directories.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command lists enabled Apache site symlinks.
when_to_use: Use when Apache ignores a virtual host file you edited.
when_not_to_use: Do not remove or create symlinks from this output unless you intend to enable or disable a site.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Enabled Apache site filenames and their symlink targets.

shorts.title: Is the Apache site enabled?
shorts.voiceover: Apache can have a perfect virtual host file that is not enabled. List the symlinks before editing more.
shorts.caption: Available is not enabled.

linkedin.question: Have you ever edited an Apache site that was not enabled?

title: Map Apache Virtual Hosts
hook: Apache chose a virtual host. You need to know which one.
problem: You need to find Apache VirtualHost blocks and their names.
starting_state: A fixture contains enabled Apache virtual host files.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command searches enabled Apache configs.
when_to_use: Use when a domain is served by the wrong Apache virtual host.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume ServerAlias coverage is complete without checking redirects and DNS too.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: VirtualHost, ServerName, and ServerAlias lines with file and line numbers.

shorts.title: Find Apache virtual hosts.
shorts.voiceover: When Apache serves the wrong site, map VirtualHost, ServerName, and ServerAlias before changing redirects.
shorts.caption: Map names to virtual hosts.

linkedin.question: When Apache serves the wrong site, what do you inspect first?

youtube.description: Map Apache virtual host names from enabled configs.

title: Find Apache Document Roots
hook: Apache was serving files from a different directory than expected.
problem: You need to list DocumentRoot values from enabled Apache configs.
starting_state: A fixture contains Apache virtual host files with document roots.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command prints DocumentRoot config lines.
when_to_use: Use when static files, index pages, or deploy output appear missing.
when_not_to_use: Do not change file permissions until you verify Apache is pointing at the expected directory.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Enabled Apache config files and DocumentRoot paths.

shorts.title: Where is Apache serving from?
shorts.voiceover: Before moving files or changing permissions, list Apache DocumentRoot lines. The web server may be looking elsewhere.
shorts.caption: Check DocumentRoot first.

linkedin.question: Do you verify DocumentRoot before fixing web file permissions?

title: Map Apache Proxy Rules
hook: Apache was up. The reverse proxy target was wrong.
problem: You need to find Apache ProxyPass and ProxyPassReverse rules.
starting_state: A fixture contains Apache reverse-proxy virtual hosts.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command lists Apache proxy directives.
when_to_use: Use when Apache returns proxy errors or forwards traffic to the wrong backend.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume the backend is healthy just because ProxyPass points to it.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Config files and Apache proxy target rules.

shorts.title: Where is Apache proxying?
shorts.voiceover: A proxy error can be a target problem. List ProxyPass rules before restarting Apache or the app.
shorts.caption: ProxyPass tells the route.

linkedin.question: For Apache proxy errors, do you check ProxyPass before the app logs?

youtube.description: List Apache ProxyPass and ProxyPassReverse rules.

title: Find Web Server Redirect Rules
hook: The redirect loop was hiding in plain text.
problem: You need to find redirect-related rules across Nginx and Apache configs.
starting_state: A fixture contains Nginx and Apache configs with redirect rules.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command searches for common redirect directives.
when_to_use: Use when browsers report too many redirects or HTTP and HTTPS behavior disagrees.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume every rewrite is a redirect; inspect status codes and conditions before editing.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Config paths and redirect or rewrite lines.

shorts.title: Find redirect rules fast.
shorts.voiceover: Redirect loops often come from one small rule. Search Nginx and Apache config before poking at DNS or certificates.
shorts.caption: Find redirects before changing them.

linkedin.question: What is your first command for a redirect loop?
linkedin.cta: Search redirect rules across web configs.

youtube.description: Find redirect and rewrite rules across Nginx and Apache configs.
